title: Wiki freshness closes over EVERY generated artifact: one page state machine, page-key orphans, and a written-bytes tamper tripwire
context: E2E audit (self-review + two independent adversarial agents) of the wiki pipeline. Privacy verdict: zero leak paths — the WikiHome source→destination pairing held everywhere. Correctness found real lifecycle holes: the README index lived outside the freshness system (repo-wide constraint changes / README deletion invisible forever), component renames and --dir changes stranded old pages that could later fire unhealable gating drift, adopted-copy pin healing used first-occurrence string replace (mangled prose ids, healed only one marker per line), --check failed CI on repos that never adopted a wiki, an empty graph made --heal unreachable while drift kept demanding it, and hand-edited pages passed --check as fresh forever.
decision: (1) ONE state machine — pageState() — grades every generated artifact (component pages, adopted copies, specs ledger, and now the README index under reserved id '_index', hashed over its real inputs: rows, repo-wide invariants, doc counts). (2) Orphans are computed by PAGE KEY (manifest entries no current artifact claims), not component-id liveness — renames, retired adoptions, and abandoned paths all retire on --heal; nothing generated is ever stranded. (3) The manifest records the written page's content hash ('bytes'): a hand-edited or merge-mangled page grades stale ("edited by hand") and --heal restores the derived view — the 'do not edit by hand' contract finally has a tripwire (optional field; pre-bytes manifests degrade gracefully). (4) Adopted-copy healing rewrites pins ONLY inside their own marker (anchored regex, all markers per line); bare decision ids in prose are never touched; the adoption hash now covers rejected alternatives (they render). (5) CLI jurisdiction guards: --check on a never-adopted repo is a green no-op (CI-safe); --dir mismatch with an adopted manifest is refused; --heal stays reachable on an empty graph for cleanup; --private refuses a degenerate overlay whose parent IS the repo root. (6) --dir is normalized to POSIX-no-trailing-slash before becoming committed manifest keys.
consequences: Every artifact answers to the same drift verb; a wiki where `hunch wiki --check` is green is now byte-faithful, complete, and unstranded — not just input-fresh
Existing manifests upgrade in place: missing '_index'/'bytes' simply grade new/skip-tamper until the next heal writes them
computeWikiDrift cost grows by one file read per otherwise-fresh page (tamper check) — bounded by page count

Known accepted residuals: relation links are not hash-covered (a slug reassignment can leave a fresh page linking to a renamed sibling until its own inputs move); local `hunch drift` output on overlay machines names private page paths (labels only — established local-output doctrine); the private index banner shows even in shared mode (conservative)
alternatives_rejected: Leaving the README outside freshness ('rewritten on any write anyway'): a repo-wide-only constraint change writes nothing, so the index staled silently forever
Orphan detection by component-id liveness (the original): correct for deletions, blind to renames and --dir moves — stranded pages later fire doc-anchor-stale that no heal can clear
Hashing page bytes as a freshness INPUT instead of a separate tamper field: would conflate 'inputs moved' with 'output touched' and break the inputs-only hashing contract
Healing pins with plain string replace on the line: first occurrence may be prose, not the marker — corrupted copy is byte-stable and therefore unhealable
